title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: The presentation focuses on the relationship between digital connections and mental well-being. It is presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student from Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: Gen Z are recognized as digital natives who experience life through an online lens. Data reveals that 95% of students use YouTube daily, and 70% engage with Instagram and Snapchat. Constant connectivity is essential in the modern era.

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: The slide presents statistics relating to digital usage and mental health impacts. 87% of users spend more than 2 hours per day online, 41% feel anxious or sad, and there is a 70% increase in depression risk.

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: Digital use leads to psychological effects such as the FOMO effect, comparison issues, and cyberbullying. Fear of missing out increases stress and anxiety, curated feeds lead to negative self-image, and 87% of teens face online harassment.

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: The slide discusses strategies to mitigate digital impact on mental well-being. Digital detox with time limits, educating on platform algorithms, and prioritizing user well-being by companies are crucial strategies.
